name: Wild Mushrom and Pasta Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, whisk milk into flour until smooth. Add dried porcini and bring to a boil over moderate heat, stirring and scraping up any flour clinging to the bottom of the pan.
2. Add salt to taste and cook over moderately low heat, stirring, about 5 minutes.
3. Pour the sauce through a sieve into a bowl, pressing hard on mushrooms, and transfer cooked mushrooms to a cutting board. Mince cooked mushrooms fine and return them to the sauce. Transfer 1/4 cup of sauce to a small saucepan and reserve.
4. Into the remaining sauce, whisk 3 tablespoons Parmesan cheese and salt and pepper to taste and reserve.
5. In a large pot, bring 4 quarts water and salt to boil for noodles. Add noodles and boil 40 seconds. Drain noodles in a colander and transfer immediately to a bowl of cold water. Drain noodles immediately and transfer to a bowl.
6. In a non-stick skillet, heat olive oil over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking. Stir in wild mushrooms. Saute mushrooms, stirring, for 1 minute and cook covered, over moderately low heat, for 3 minutes or until wilted and tender. Increase heat to moderately high and saute mushrooms, uncovered, stirring, until all liquid from the mushrooms is evaporated.
7. Stir mushrooms into noodles.
8. Preheat oven to 375°F and spray a 9-inch round baking dish or gratin dish with cooking spray.
9. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat egg whites with a pinch of salt until they just hold stiff peaks. Partially fold the whites into the sauce in the bowl and gently fold in the noodle mixture. Transfer to the baking dish and sprinkle with the remaining 2 tablespoons Parmesan cheese.
10. Bake in the middle of the oven for 25 minutes or until just set in the center and slightly puffed.
11. Preheat a broiler. Add just enough reserved sauce to reach the consistency of heavy cream and heat until heated through. Broil souffle about 4 inches from the heat until crisp and golden, about 30 seconds.
12. Serve souffle with a small amount of sauce spooned around each serving.
